hypothesis_updates.evidence_summary: More than one production compose or deployment entrypoint can claim canonical authority.
The current release unit does not bind all five required services to one exact Git SHA and immutable image digest set.
A partial topology can currently pass health or smoke checks.
At least one repair path can create a new effect identity rather than reconciling the original identity.
Rollback is service-local and can leave code, images, configuration, or migrations on different release identities.
Backup and restore do not prove refindable owner proof after database and filesystem recovery.
Secret versions are not included in restore or release proof, allowing technically successful recovery with stale or incompatible credentials.
Host Provisioner is not the sole transitive Docker authority.
Manager or Model Gateway can attach to employee networks without an explicit scoped authority record.
Production egress is broader than required and lacks an inspectable bounded policy.
Temporal decisions mix process clock and database clock, permitting stale lease, expiry, or retry behavior under skew.
Application code rewrites or normalizes database-authoritative returned records instead of validating them.
Fault injection uses unstable line or timing hooks rather than named crash points bound to semantic transitions.
Release provenance is self-asserted and lacks an independent verifier with a separate trust boundary.
Build reproducibility is not checked by rebuilding the same exact SHA and comparing declared artifacts.
The current CI does not build and inspect Manager, Gateway, Provisioner, Web, and Caddy as one release candidate.
Capacity harnesses count requests but omit open connections, SSE clients, queues, per-employee fairness, and noisy-neighbor effects.
Saturation can render as generic failure or healthy degradation without an explicit operator-visible state.
Pilot controls lack machine-readable stop authority, rollback target, incident owner, data boundary, and customer exit criteria.
The inherited WS-07 migration still contains an unqualified rate-window update that fails under PL/pgSQL output-column namespace rules.
